objective: Replay a synthetic authorized K-12 storage exposure assessment with public client config and unauthenticated student-support file access.
assumptions: The lab uses synthetic evidence only.
No real external target is contacted.
Student-support records, buckets, and URLs are intentionally fake.

phases.objective: Map public storage configuration and downloadable file paths.
actions: Load the synthetic client configuration endpoint
Identify whether bucket names, object prefixes, and signed download URLs are exposed without authentication
successCriteria: Storage configuration exposure evidence is recorded with a durable evidence ID
noSubagents: student-data handling and disclosure decisions stay with the primary operator

actions: Replay a synthetic unauthenticated download for a student-support export
Record the missing authentication and authorization control
successCriteria: A report-ready finding cites both the leaked config and the unauthenticated file read evidence
noSubagents: final privacy impact wording stays with the primary operator

evidence.title: Synthetic public storage configuration response
evidence.summary: The client configuration endpoint disclosed a storage bucket, student-support prefix, and unauthenticated download URL template.

evidence.title: Synthetic unauthenticated student-support file download
evidence.summary: An unauthenticated request downloaded a synthetic student-support export containing sensitive education-support fields.

findings.title: Unauthenticated access to student-support storage files
findings.description: The synthetic application discloses storage object paths and allows unauthenticated download of a student-support export.
findings.impact: An unauthenticated user could access sensitive education-support data, increasing privacy, compliance, and student-safety risk.
findings.remediation: Keep bucket names and privileged prefixes out of public config when possible, require authenticated server-side authorization before generating downloads, and enforce object-level access rules for student-support files.
